content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
Sacramento. CA 958 12-0807
(916)322-5660
• Fax (916) 322-0886
June 12, 1998
Mark W. Shurtleff
Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP
Jamboree Center
4 Park Plaza
Irvine, California 926 14-8557
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-98-139
Dear Mr. Shurtleff:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Donald W. Murphy and
Patrick Sanger regarding Section 87406 of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1 We are
declining to provide written advice since your request for advice relates to past conduct because
both Mr. Murphy and Mr. Sanger have been with the Hearst San Simeon Monument Foundation
since early 1998 and because they may be limited in their activities by the permanent ban in
Sections 87400 to 87405 of the Act, but you have specifically requested not to receive advice in
that regard. See Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), (b)(8)(F), (c)(4)(A), and (c)(4)(G) (copy enclosed).
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
MarteCastafios
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:MC:jlw
Enclosure
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Donald W. Murphy and Patrick Sanger are subject to restrictions under Section 87406 of the Political Reform Act, particularly given their employment with the Hearst San Simeon Monument Foundation since early 1998 and potential limitations from the permanent ban in Sections 87400 to 87405.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request relates to past conduct and the requestor specifically declined advice regarding the permanent ban provisions (Sections 87400-87405) that may limit the clients' activities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document clearly states the FPPC is declining to provide written advice and cites specific regulatory bases for the declination. Document structure indicates this was provided in response to a prior request from the attorney.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Donald W. Murphy and Patrick Sanger are subject to restrictions under Section 87406 of the Political Reform Act, particularly given their employment with the Hearst San Simeon Monument Foundation since early 1998 and potential limitations from the permanent ban in Sections 87400 to 87405.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request relates to past conduct and the requestor specifically declined advice regarding the permanent ban provisions (Sections 87400-87405) that may limit the clients' activities.

Murphy and Patrick Sanger regarding Section 87406 of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 We are declining to provide written advice since your request for advice relates to past conduct because both Mr. Murphy and Mr. Sanger have been with the Hearst San Simeon Monument Foundation since early 1998 and because they may be limited in their activities by the permanent ban in Sections 87400 to 87405 of the Act, but you have specifically requested not to receive advice in that regard. See Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), (b)(8)(F), (c)(4)(A), and (c)(4)(G) (copy enclosed). If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. Sincerely, Steven G. embedding.summary: FPPC declination letter denying written advice to Mark Shurtleff (Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher) regarding Section 87406 of the Political Reform Act as it applies to Donald W. Murphy and Patrick Sanger, citing that the request involves past conduct and the requestor's exclusion of advice on the permanent ban limitations.
